reviews.review_description: Great app and selection of games. My husband and I particularly like the board games (chess,  backgammon, etc.). Would love more of those types of games added. One minor gripe about multi-player, we don't see a way to "play again" quickly. We have to exit, create another room, join, etc. just to play the same game again. We would have played archery over and over against each other, but only played 3 times because of the room hassle.

Saw this game as something I could play with my family overseas. We all loved the game choices and when initially playing it it didn’t disappoint. I really love the game choices and the fact you can alter the height and in game position if you want to play sitting down.

Now here’s the but. Initially played a game with one relative and we were having fun, but when another relative tried to join our room he couldn’t. It seems like multiplayer is only 2 people. I understand there are 2 player games, eg chess, checkers, tic tac toe etc but there are also games like ludo which is for 4 people but only 2 are allowed ?? There are 3 family members so one of us is left out. Could this be changed

There are only a few VR games that focus on old school chill board type games like checkers, ect. This VR game does the job very well...
Better than another "mini"multi game in App Lab that has checkers and almost made me give up hope in App Lab.Glad I got The Master Club.

reviews.review_title: Love this, except for one limitation
reviews.review_description:
This game is so much fun and offers great value for money, however I am disappointed that the multiplayer update only supports two players. My wife and I live in the US, and my sister in the UK. We are always looking for multiplayer games that the three of us can play together, closing the miles between us and bring us closer together virtually. After reading about this app, and the multiplayer feature, all three of us bought it together. It was so disappointing to discover that the game only supported two players.

Whilst understandably, some game like chess and checkers can only have two players, we were expecting that other games without this fundamental limitation could be enjoyed by up to four players, as most Quest games. This would allow us to connect to other friends on both sides of the pond and enjoy these really well put together games.

Hopefully we can see some future enhancements to make this really good app perfect.

I really like these games. They are comfortable and fun to play. The only issue I have is that you can't have more than 2 players. Yes, I know that multi-player means more than 1,  but I was hoping for more. 

Ludo, for example, shows 4 players on the gameboard, but only 2 people can be in a room. My husband and I play together, and we convinced my sister-in-law to also purchase it in hopes that the 3 of us could play together. Sadly, we can not.  We still have fun playing 2 at a time, but it just isn't the same.

As a suggestion,  I would love to see this app become multi-player, in the sense that it is up to 4 players in a room, as Ludo suggests. I can not fault the app, though. It is great. 

If we could all 3 play together, I would happily change my rating to a 5 star review.
Graphics are nice, you have many games and since the update you can play on multiplayer mode which makes this game even more fun!!!

12 minigames in one $5 game is a great deal! Air hockey alone is worth the buy! I think that's my favorite one from the entire pack. The physics work really well, heck I pay $5 just to play it for 5 minutes in the arcade. Archery was a lot of fun too, as well as mini gold, checkers, chess, billiards, and bowling. What really surprised me is playing solitaire in VR! A few games, like basketball and table tennis, need some physics adjustment. Overall, if the developer adds multiplayer to the game it will be a great game collection on Quest 2. Well done!
It's pretty good. The fingers really need to bend to show grabbing, though. The fact that the hands are just locked in a single position feels cheap and lacks immersion.

developer_response.body: You should try update 2.01 now your hands show also grabbing animations.

reviews.review_description: You won't be sorry for buying it.

reviews.review_title: A great start, esp for newbies
reviews.review_description:
I thought for five bucks I'd give this a go...overall I thought it was a pretty darn good effort, however there were a couple of weak points...but we also have to remember that App Lab stuff is not necessarily final stage, and I'd consider this a very well done Beta.  The static games (solitaire, chess, checkers) were excellent...and I agree with your comment about having the AI pause for a moment or two before taking its turn, maybe slowing down the animation a little or adding a floating hand would be a nice tweak.  I found the rubiks cube controls a little clunky but usable.  The air hockey was a lot of fun, top points there, as was the archery.  It was wanting a bit I thought in the other games...table tennis wasn't quite there, and the mini golf physics need a tweak to make the ball travel more realistically as opposed to feeling like it is in sand.  I couldn't get the basketball to throw properly, and the bowling wasn't quite there either...close though, maybe a bit more haptic work might help that.  Billiards was pretty good too, and showcased how bad I am at it.
Overall, I think that there are enough good games in here to warrant the spend.  If the devs make a couple of physics tweaks to the action games and one or two minor playability enhancements, that will elevate it into a must-have, especially for the younglings and those starting out in VR.
waste of money not good at all

developer_response.body:
Thanks for your comment,
Maybe future updates of the game will fit better your expectations.

reviews.review_description: Fun....But i wish you could see your arrow shoot out, instead of being stuck in the bow after shooting! Also i wish you could see the hands and fingers bend when you grasp something.  Great set of games!! Can't wait for some updates!

developer_response.body: In the update 2.01 now your hands show also grabbing animations.

reviews.review_description:
Everything about it is Great. I really enjoy it. But many of these are multi player. Would be nice to have friends be able to play with you. I'm not talking about online match making. Just invite only. 
I would have everyone I know buy this game if that was the case

developer_response.body: You should try update 2.01 now you have a matchmaking system to play online against other human players.

reviews.review_title: A nice collection, good for what it is.
reviews.review_description:
I've been looking for casual games for when friends are over and want to have a go on the quest 2.  Something that is familiar to people, easy to pick up and play and isn't too frantic that it will cause any kind of dizziness or motion sickness.  This seemed to fit the bill.

When I first tried 'The Master Club' I wasn't so sure, the controls seemed clunky and I didn't have much fun at all with any of the games.  I later tried the games out again and found that I had a lot more fun.  As of the original date of this review: 16/7/22, there are 12 games in total.  The last is a card game called 'Solitaire/Klondike'.  There is also an extra scenario not listed called 'Hotel'.  The controls are easy to learn, and you can view them for each game via the menu button and selecting 'Controls' whilst playing the game.  Just remember to click the arrows either side of the image as it is a manual slide show and wasn't so obvious to me.

Some people will say the physics isn't perfect but all the various mechanics work how you would expect them to.  And for £3.99 you really can't grumble, I've had a lot of fun out of this collection and I'm sure when I have 5 mins to kill I'll jump into one of these again as this is a 'no frills easy to pick up collection of games that are fun to play', and will be perfect for when friends are round.  Nice to note that the dev's are working to improve on this collection of games.
